YF 3202 I 2202 BOARD DIRECTORS H.E Ato Solomon Desta Chairperson Ato Melaku Kebede Vice Chairperson Ato Asfaw Alemu Ato Daniel Tekeste Ato Dereje Zebene Board Director Board Director Board Director Ato Girum Tsegaye Ato Teferi Mekonnen Ato Yehuala Gessesse Board Director Board Director Board Director Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch I

BOARD CHAIRPERSON’S MESSAGE The financial year 2022/2023 was marred by challenges both nationally and globally. Despite the many factors that adversely affected the global and local economic environment, EthSwitch has achieved significant milestones. During the concluded financial period, EthSwitch successfully implemented its strategic plan, achieving notable advancements in policy, income generation, project implemnetation, and operational efficiency. Ethswitch has earned a profit before tax of Birr 534,534,327; accordingly, the pre-tax profit has increased by 172%, a record-high figure compared to the previous years. This achievement is realized by the unwavering commitment to enhance operational efficiency, revenue growth, and cost optimization. Besides, this achievement is attributed to the overall effort of the board of directors, management, and staff. Moreover, Ethswitch is playing a significant role in the realization of National Digital Payment Stratgy, a strategy to promote e-commerce and digital services at the national level, and the National Financial Inclusion Strategy II, which aims to create universal access to affordable financial services and products by 2025. These national strategies are strongly aligned with the strategic aspirations and goals of Ethswitch. EthSwitch has succeeded in various service areas, including Person-to-Person (P2P) services, ATM and POS transaction interoperability, and card and PIN Esteemed Shareholders, personalization services. These accomplishments reveal the company’s contributions to the modernization of the On behalf of the Board of Directors and myself, I am national payment system and the expansion of financial pleased to present EthSwitch’s Annual Report for the inclusion nationwide. Financial Year 2022/2023. Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch II

EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT Yilebes Addis Chief Executive Officer Yosef Melka Nebiyu Mengistu Assefa Yeshanew Chief Corporate Services Chief Operations Officer Chief Information Officer Officer Solomon Mohammed Beza Mamo Abeneazer Wondwossen Director, IT Infrastructure Director, Payment Applications Director, Program Management Department Department Office Yared Seyoum Yoseph Endale Director, Strategy & Change Director, Marketing Department Management Department Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch IV

CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER’S MESSAGE Esteemed Shareholders, I am delighted to report to you the achievements of EthSwitch during the fiscal year 2022/23. We have made significant progress in operational activities, technology, revenue generation, project execution, and international engagements, making it a truly remarkable year for EthSwitch. As the National Switch, EthSwitch plays a crucial role in simplifying payments, and I am pleased to highlight the key accomplishments of the past fiscal year: 1. Interoperable ATM Transactions: We conducted an impressive 71,433,041 successful interoperable ATM transactions, providing convenient and seamless payment options to our customers across Ethiopia. 2. Person-to-Person (P2P) Interoperable Service: Our P2P interoperable service achieved immense success, totaling 14,140,881 transactions. This service has empowered individuals to transfer funds easily and securely, contributing to financial inclusion. 3. Interoperable POS Purchases: We have achived 970,434 successful transactions for interoperable POS purchases, enabling individuals and businesses to efficiently complete their transactions. 4. Real-Time Payment (RTP) Project: We made significant progress in the Real-Time Payment project, including successfully deploying the shared reconciliation system for EthSwitch. This project has enhanced the speed and efficiency of payment processing, benefiting our customers and stakeholders. 5. Strategic Plan: successfully conducted mid-term review and revision of our strategic plan, and excellent progress has been made in implementing the third-year strategic plan. This strategic focus has guided our initiatives and paved the way for our success. Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch V

Thank you for your continued trust and investment Furthermore, I am delighted to report substantial in EthSwitch. Your support is invaluable, and we are improvements compared to the previous fiscal year: enthusiastic about the opportunities that lie ahead. • Interoperable transactions increased by an impressive 130%, reflecting the growing adoption of digital payments in Ethiopia. Thank you. • Our total assets increased by a remarkable 64%, showcasing our steady growth and financial strength. • Net profit before tax surged by an outstanding 172%, a testament to our successful revenue generation Yilebes Addis and sound financial management. Chief Executive Officer Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch VI

OUR CORPORATE PHILOSOPHY Strategic Aspirations To scale up digital payment transactions in Ethiopia by being the best-in-class switch that enables all value chain participants. Strategic Goals-Vision 2025 Create Interoperability Across All Channels and Players Annual Report 2022I2023 I EthSwitch VII 1 laoG 2 laoG 3 laoG 4 laoG 5 laoG EthSwitch is the National Switch Owned by all commercial banks, the National Bank of Ethiopia, MFIs (Microfinance Institutions), PIIs (Payment Instrument Issuers), and PSOs (Payment System Operators). EthSwitch is mandated to Create Interoperability between financial institutions, Implement a Domestic Payment Scheme, provide National Payment Gateway, serve as Central Interconnectivity for clearing & settlement, and provide Shared Platform /Infrastructure services. Mission Making Payments Simple and Affordable. Vision Develop Payment Scheme To be the Best-in-Class Payment Network in Africa by 2035. Provide Shared Platform and Infrastructure Services Core Values Build Internal Dynamic Capability Integrity Security Innovative Ensure Service Excellence: Secure, Reliable, Seamless Services Customer Focused Enabling Employee Focused

Annual Report 2022 I 2023 BOARD OF DIRECTORS’ REPORT Ethiopia’s digital payment landscape is rapidly evolving, driven by improved telecom infrastructure, government support, competition, economic growth, and urbanization. This transformation brings tangible changes, including increased ATMs and POS terminals, development in mobile banking and Internet banking, and EthSwitch’s role in facilitating interoperable payments. These changes have positive impacts, enhancing convenience, efficiency, security, and financial inclusion. The government’s commitment to the National Digital Payments Strategy (NDPS) further promotes this development. However, challenges such as financial literacy, security, and regulatory frameworks must be addressed. EthSwitch’s role as the national payment switch operator is crucial in advancing digital Figure 1.1: Growth of Industry-Wide ATM & POS Terminals Deployment, 2018/19-2022/23 1 aipoihtE fo knaB lanoitaN :ecruoS The Board of Directors of EthSwitch is honored to present the EthSwitch Annual Report and Audited Financial Statements for the Financial Year ended June 30, 2023, to the 10th Annual General Meeting of its Shareholders. During the reporting period, EthSwitch continued to deliver superior operational and financial performances and remarkable project implementation despite the challenging year due to domestic and global issues. 1. Digital Payments Landscape payments. The government and private sector must collaborate, prioritize security, and invest further to unlock the full potential of digital payments in Ethiopia. With continued support, Ethiopia can become a prominent player in Africa’s digital payments market. According to data obtained from the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE), in 2022/23, there was a total of 7,858 Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) deployed all over the country, while there were 12,016 Point of Sales (POS) terminals active and deployed on merchant premises.

The total number of ATM transactions has reached 356,384,957 in the banking sector, with the corresponding value of Birr 478,319,000,000. Annual Report 2022 I 2023 4. Strategy and Other Development 4.1. Strategy Development and Revision In FY 2020/21, EthSwitch S.C. began implementing its fiscal year. Accordingly, EthSwitch has established five first five-year strategic plan. Subsequent strategic plan strategic goals to effectively address the gaps that re- review and revision were conducted in the 2022/23 quire attention during the strategic period spanning from 2020/21 to 2024/25. Create Interoperability across All Channels and Players: EthSwitch will provide interoperability across all digital retail channels and technologies within the strategic period. Through the achievements of these goals, EthSwitch the Best-in-Class Payment Network in Africa by has reset its mission and vision, which are: 2035,” respectively. “Making Payments Simple & Affordable” and “To Be 11 1 laoG Ensure Service Excellence: Secure, Reliable, and Seamless Services: EthSwitch will provide a high-quality, reliable, and seamless service with low failure rates across all digital transaction channels. This will require EthSwitch to implement a clear set of SLAs for each service and commit to continuous innovation and development. As a measurable output, EthSwitch can measure its SLA attainment levels and compare them to leading regional switches. 5 laoG Develop a Payment Scheme: EthSwitch will develop the domestic card scheme, EthioPay, with its own scheme rules to be used for domestic payments across all channels. 2 laoG Provide Shared Platform and Infrastructure Services: EthSwitch will provide shared platform and infrastructure services to the financial institutions in the country. Through a collective investment by EthSwitch, these financial institutions will receive these services in a cost-effective and technically efficient way. 3 laoG Build Internal Dynamic Capability: EthSwitch will build its institutional capacity in terms of human resources, technologies, processes, and knowledge to be able to provide a unified e-payment platform for all financial institutions in Ethiopia while managing risks.

As a measurable outcome/result, the institutionalization of enterprise risk management framework, deployment of relevant state-of-the-art technologies and systems, deployment of high-caliber personnel, as well as the establishment of conducive working facilities are some of them. 4 laoG

Annual Report 2022 I 2023 Tafesse, Shisema and Ayalew Certified Audit Partnership Chartered Certified Accountants (UK) and authorized Auditors (Ethiopia) Member Firm of HlB International THE GLOBAL ADVISORY AND ACCOUNTING NETWORK Tel.251-011-8961752/0116180638 Mob.0911229425 / 0930034356 / 0930034357 Fax: 251-011 662 12 70/60 E-mail: tafessef@gmail.com / tafessef@hotmail.com P.O.Box 110690 Addis Ababa-Ethiopia INDEPENDENT AUDITOR’S REPORT ON THE ACCOUNTS OF ETHSWITCH SHARE COMPANY Opinion We have audited the financial statements of Eth Switch Share Company, which comprise the statement of financial position as at 30 June 2023, the Statement of profit or loss and other comprehensive income, the statement of changes in equity and statement of cash flow for the year then ended, and notes to the financial statements, including a summary of significant accounting policies and other explanatory information. In our opinion, the accompanying financial statements present fairly, in all material respects the financial position of Eth Switch Share Company as at 30 June 2023, and the results of its operations and its cash flows for the year then ended in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRSs) issued by the International Accounting Standard Board. Basis for Opinion We conducted our audit in accordance with International Standards on Auditing (ISAs). Our responsibilities under those standards are further described in the Auditor’s Responsibilities for the Audit of the Financial Statements section of our report. We are independent of the company in accordance with the International Ethics Standards Board for Accountants Code of Ethics for Professional Accountants (IESBA Code) together with the ethical requirements that are relevant to our audit of the financial statements in Ethiopia, and we have fulfilled our other ethical responsibilities in accordance with these requirements. We believe that the audit evidence we have obtained is sufficient and appropriate to provide a basis for our opinion.

Responsibilities of Directors for the Financial Statements The Directors of the company are responsible for the preparation and fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards, and for such internal control as management determines is necessary to enable the preparation of financial statements that are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error. In preparing the financial statements, Directors are responsible for assessing the Company’s ability to continue as a going concern, disclosing, as applicable, matters related to going concern and using the going concern basis of accounting unless the Directors either intends to liquidate the company or to cease operations, or have no realistic alternative but to do so. The Directors are responsible for overseeing the Company’s financial reporting process. 26

Annual Report 2022 I 2023 Auditor’s Responsibilities for the Audit of the Financial Statements Our objectives are to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the financial statements as a whole are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error, and to issue an auditor’s report that includes our opinion. Reasonable assurance is a high level of assurance, but is not a guarantee that an audit conducted in accordance with ISAs will always detect a material misstatement when it exists. Misstatements can arise from fraud or error and are considered material if, individually or in the aggregate, they could reasonably be expected to influence the economic decisions of users taken on the basis of these financial statements. Report on Other Legal and Regulatory Requirements We have no comment to make on the report of your director’s report so far as it relates to these financial statements and pursuant to article 349 (1&2) of the commercial code of Ethiopia of 2021 ( Proclamation No. 1243/2021, we recommend approval of the mentioned financial statements. In addition, we have no objection on the amount of dividend proposed by the directors and hence in accordance with article 349 (3) of the commercial code of Ethiopia of 2021 recommended approval of the proposed dividend distribution. Tafesse, Shisema and Ayalew (TMS Plus) Certified Audit Partnership Chartered Certified Accountants (UK) Addis Ababa Authorized Auditors (ETH) 07 September 2023 27
